parentage.mortal: Aeson - King of Thessaly; Polymele - Mother

keyMyths.title: The Quest for the Golden Fleece
keyMyths.description: Jason, rightful heir to the throne of Iolcus, embarks on a perilous journey to Colchis to retrieve the Golden Fleece, a symbol of power, to reclaim his birthright. He assembles a crew of heroes, the Argonauts, for this dangerous expedition.

keyMyths.title: The Sowing of the Dragon's Teeth
keyMyths.description: In Colchis, King Aeetes demands Jason complete impossible tasks, including yoking fire-breathing oxen and sowing dragon's teeth, from which sprung armed warriors. Medea, daughter of Aeetes, aids Jason in overcoming these challenges.

keyMyths.title: The Capture of the Golden Fleece
keyMyths.description: With Medea's assistance, Jason successfully retrieves the Golden Fleece, outwitting the dragon guarding it. They then flee Colchis, pursued by Aeetes and his forces, facing further trials on their return journey.

keyMyths.title: The Murder of Pelias
keyMyths.description: Upon returning to Iolcus, Jason seeks revenge on his uncle Pelias, who had usurped the throne and killed Jason's father. Medea uses her sorcery to trick Pelias' daughters into killing him, though Jason does not directly participate.

keyMyths.title: Jason's Broken Vows and Death
keyMyths.description: Jason abandons Medea to marry Glauce, the daughter of King Creon of Corinth. This betrayal leads to Medea's vengeful acts, including the murder of Glauce and her own children with Jason. Jason eventually dies alone and unhappy, struck by a piece of the Argo.

achievements: Assembled the Argonauts, a team of legendary heroes.
Sailed the Argo through treacherous seas and encountered numerous mythical creatures.
Yoked the fire-breathing oxen of Colchis.
Sowed the dragon's teeth and defeated the earthborn warriors.
Acquired the Golden Fleece with the help of Medea.
Escaped from Colchis with the Golden Fleece, overcoming pursuit.
Avenged his father's death by orchestrating the downfall of Pelias (indirectly).

sources.name: Apollonius Rhodius, Argonautica
sources.description: The most comprehensive and detailed account of the Argonauts' voyage and the quest for the Golden Fleece.
sources.title: Apollonius Rhodius, Argonautica

sources.name: Apollodorus, Bibliotheca
sources.description: A valuable source for Greek myths, providing a concise summary of Jason's life and adventures.
sources.title: Apollodorus, Bibliotheca

sources.name: Hyginus, Fabulae
sources.description: A collection of mythological stories, including a version of the Jason myth with some unique details.
sources.title: Hyginus, Fabulae

sources.name: Euripides, Medea
sources.description: A tragic play focusing on Medea's perspective and her revenge on Jason for his betrayal.
sources.title: Euripides, Medea

sources.name: Diodorus Siculus, Bibliotheca Historica
sources.description: A universal history that includes accounts of mythological events, offering another perspective on Jason's story.
sources.title: Diodorus Siculus, Bibliotheca Historica
